title: Andrew Matheson
content:
People.ContactEmail amatheson@mccarthy.ca
- University of Victoria
Andrew Matheson is the Head of our National White Collar and Investigations Group and a partner in our Litigation Group in Toronto. He specializes in complex and cross-border investigations, regulatory and criminal defence, and professional discipline matters.
Andrew has diverse advocacy experience in sensitive litigation and investigations. Andrew has represented clients at all levels of the Ontario court and before various administrative and disciplinary tribunals in the province. He has conducted investigation relating to a wide range of exposures, including fraud, corruption, insider trading, money laundering and tax evasion. Andrew’s experience includes practising securities litigation in New York City with Allen & Overy for four years. He was a member of the McCarthy Tétrault team that successfully defended the former CEO of Nortel on criminal fraud charges. Andrew also frequently defends physicians and prosecutes teachers in professional disciplinary matters.
Andrew is active in pro bono work, assisting underprivileged clients in criminal, immigration, commercial and other matters. His experience also includes acting for Guantanamo Bay detainees in habeas corpus litigation. Andrew is an adjunct professor at University of Toronto Law School, teaching White Collar Crime in Global Context. Andrew is a contributor to Annotated Ontario Securities Legislation , Annotated British Columbia Securities Legislation published by LexisNexis.
Upon his graduation from the University of Victoria Law School in 1995, Andrew was awarded the McIntyre Prize for academic excellence, leadership and community involvement. He completed his LLM at Columbia University in 2003, graduating as a Stone Scholar, and started his career working with Edward L. Greenspan, QC, defending criminal prosecutions.

#### Representative Cases
- Guided company client through sports industry investigation and parallel criminal investigation
- Developed anti-foreign interference, security, and integrity policies for a major financial institution
- Advised a public company on Canadian implications of U.S. prosecution and eventual deferred prosecution agreement
- Secured withdrawal of criminal obstruction charge against a bank manager
- Negotiated the avoidance of criminal fraud charges against client following Ontario Police investigation
- Assisted financial institution in Canadian law enforcement and civil matters arising from U.S. money laundering allegations
- Secured withdrawal of criminal charge against client in alleged cyber attack
- Advised public officials in law enforcement investigations
- Conducted employee fraud investigation resulting in judgment and criminal charges against perpetrators
- Led an ethics and compliance review of an engineering firm operating around the globe
- Secured the acquittal of a physician in a criminal trial
- Coordinated an investigation and regulatory response for a multinational resources enterprise in respect of corruption and securities law allegations arising in multiple jurisdictions
- Advised major Canadian financial institution on worldwide investigations arising from the ‘Panama Papers’ leak’
- Obtained judgement in Ontario against a hacker for Tesla Motors
- Exonerated the target of an Ontario Securities Commission (OSC) insider trading investigation
- Obtained a stay of criminal price-fixing charges brought against a corporate defendant
- Advised on successful investigation and asset recovery in relation to employee fraud committed against a public company
- Obtained dismissal of Provincial Offences Act charges against a drilling company arising from a fatality at a mine
- Obtained emergency injunctions to remove trespassers from resource sector client assets
- Assisted a multinational company’s compliance with a deferred prosecution agreement
- Defended the former CEO of Nortel, acquitted of criminal fraud charges
- Successfully represented a financial institution accused of breaching privacy in the course of anti-money laundering investigation
- Acted for a major financial institution in connection with an investigation into the failure of the third-party asset-backed commercial paper market
- Represented Mason Capital at the OSC hearing concerning Magna International Inc.'s buyout of Frank Stronach's multiple voting shares
- Brought an application before the OSC on behalf of a minority shareholder challenging related party transactions by MI Developments Inc.
- Successfully obtained a motion to dismiss claims against U. S. Steel in a $1.8-billion action brought by ArcerlorMittal Dofasco
- Successfully defended a financial institution criminally charged with failure to respond to production orders
